meta.subject: Art and Design
meta.philosophy: The Art and Design Syllabus for Ordinary Level Secondary Education has been reviewed to provide a national age-appropriate curriculum for learners from Form 1 to 4. The syllabus aims to provide quality education that is aligned with the Competence Based Curriculum and twenty first Century Skills. It also aims to impart the knowledge, skills, values and positive attitudes that are aimed at enabling learners to live and grow into productive and useful members of their communities and the Zambian society.
meta.competence_learning: This syllabus is designed to provide necessary competences which include knowledge, positive attitudes, values and skills in specific areas of study in Art and Design. Learners are encouraged to exhibit creativity, imagination and innovation. It is not only aimed at developing the learners power of imagination and observation but also the sense of beauty and neatness.
goals: Develop analytical thinking by selecting and controlling materials, processes and techniques in an appropriate way
Enhance communication through understanding the language of Art relating to elements and principles
Foster creativity and innovation through drawing and painting from memory, imagination and observation
Build collaboration by participating fully in group tasks
Develop critical thinking through making critical judgements and showing appreciation of cultural awareness
Promote environmental sustainability through recycling waste materials
Build financial literacy including setting financial goals and understanding income and budgeting
Foster entrepreneurship through generating ideas for products, services, and projects

subtopics: 2.1.4 Secular, Religious and Functional Craft items
specific_outcomes: 2.1.2.1 Critique works of the Zambian Visual Artists

2.2.2.1 Paint using a chosen colour scheme
